danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing army units displayed on grid map with distinct palette/color coding

danger_signals.signal: Dark dragon or possessed leader appearance
danger_signals.visual_form: Medeus and later Hardin (when possessed by Darksphere) rendered with dark/corrupted visual treatment
danger_signals.meaning: Major story threat and primary objective target

danger_signals.visual_form: Hardin's military units occupying territories across Archanea continent
danger_signals.meaning: Political danger; forced military expansion and loss of autonomy

danger_signals.visual_form: Marth's army displaced from homeland, hunted across desert regions
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of status, forced retreat, betrayal by allied faction

safety_signals.signal: Allied unit roster and support relationships
safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly units (Caeda, Jagen, Gotoh) displayed on player's side of battlefield; story cutscenes showing character bonds
safety_signals.meaning: Trustworthy allies; stat boosts through Support system

safety_signals.visual_form: Falchion (magical sword) and Fire Emblem shield acquisition marked narratively
safety_signals.meaning: Legitimacy, divine right, power against dark forces

safety_signals.visual_form: Desert of Khadein and Gotoh's sanctuary serve as safe havens
safety_signals.meaning: Safe retreat points; locations for planning and recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Communication through cutscenes; Fire Emblem delivered via trusted messenger
safety_signals.meaning: Legitimate authority and support; bridge to true power

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Falchion sword; Fire Emblem shield; Altean royal heritage
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Kingdom of Altea (homeland); later scattered across Archanea, hunted in Khadein desert

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fire Emblem (royal artifact); Archanea royal crown/authority
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hardin's occupation forces (visually shift from ally to enemy forces across Book 2)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Archanea capitol and surrounding territories; symbols of legitimate rule

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dark dragon form; Manakete shapeshifting tribe identity
Hardin's royal status corrupted by Darksphere artifact; dark possession influence
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hardin rendered with corruption/dark visual indicators when possessed
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Archanea territories under military occupation; forced expansionism
Neighboring kingdom; initial invasion force against Altea

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Book 1: War and Dark Sorcery - Medeus's influence spreading
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Marth's army visually grows through recruitment; maps show expanding dark dragon influence; Gharnef and Medeus rendered with dark magical aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units engage in progressive defensive battles; NPCs demonstrate fear of dark forces; Marth's journey drives toward confrontation with Medeus

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Book 1: Peace Restored - Marth defeats Medeus and recovers Fire Emblem
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Narrative shift from dark to stabilized; Hardin ascends to throne; Falchion and Fire Emblem secured as symbols of restored order
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition cutscene showing Hardin's coronation; restoration narrative; apparent stability

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Book 2: Corruption of Authority - Hardin possessed by Darksphere, begins hostile expansion
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Archanea forces shift from allied to enemy units; Hardin's aesthetic changes to reflect dark possession; occupation forces spread across continent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Marth betrayed and hunted; forced to flee; allies become enemies; military aggression spreads across territories

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Book 2: Flight and Recovery - Marth escapes, seeks countermeasures
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert of Khadein provides visual sanctuary; Gotoh's appearance signals refuge; quest for magical spheres (Lightsphere, Lifesphere, Geosphere, Starsphere) drives visual narrative progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units regroup in exile; Nyna secretly supports Marth through courier; mountains and icy terrain become visual obstacles/journey markers

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Political Betrayal - Marth branded traitor despite heroic victory
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Status change indicated narratively through cutscenes; Marth's army hunted by former allies; visual isolation and pursuit mechanics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs treat Marth as enemy; Hardin's forces relentlessly pursue; support network disrupted except for true believers

environmental_storytelling.example: Hardin's military expansion across Archanea continent shown through maps depicting occupied vs. free territories; visual markers of forced occupation
Falchion (hero's sword, only Marth can wield due to Anri ancestry) and Fire Emblem shield serve as visual proof of legitimacy, divine right, and authority
Hardin's visual appearance shifts when possessed by Darksphere; dark visual indicators show corruption of once-trusted ally
Desert of Khadein and icy mountains serve as both literal obstacles and visual markers of exile, forcing narrative through geography
Enemy units display distinct visual styling on grid-based maps; Marth's recruited units show unified visual identity vs. enemy forces
Story conversations and Support system cutscenes show character bonds; visual intimacy indicates trust relationships that translate to mechanical stat boosts

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Recruits allies, grows army, drives toward recovering Falchion and confronting dark forces
Flees to desert sanctuary, seeks magical countermeasures, gathers scattered allies against former friends
Works alongside Marth against dark forces; ascends legitimately to throne
Initiates aggressive military expansion; hunts Marth; wields corrupted authority
Commands sorcerous forces; possesses Manakete shapeshifting tribe; leads invasion through proxy Gharnef
Provides support; becomes Hardin's wife and queen
Secretly sends Fire Emblem to Marth via courier; maintains hidden loyalty despite husband's possession
Guides Marth, reveals Darksphere corruption, provides sanctuary in Khadein, directs quest for magical spheres
Executes Medeus's will; spreads dark influence; confronted and defeated alongside Medeus
Secretly gave Darksphere to Hardin; continues dark machinations through possession
Maintains unwavering support; story-based relationship with Marth boosts stats through Support system
Fight alongside Marth against dark forces and invaders
Hunt Marth as traitor; enforce occupation across continent; visual enemies despite prior alliance

notes: Fire Emblem: Monshou no Nazo communicates world-state primarily through narrative-driven cutscenes combined with grid-based tactical battlefield visuals. The game's central story mechanic involves a trust/betrayal inversion: initial allies (Hardin, Archanea forces) become antagonists in Book 2, visually manifested through unit palette shifts and behavioral changes. Artifact symbolism (Falchion, Fire Emblem) anchors legitimacy and divine right narratives. Support system cutscenes visualize character relationships, functioning as both story and mechanical systems. Environmental geography (desert exile, icy mountains) reinforces narrative themes of displacement and struggle. The game demonstrates sophisticated political storytelling: showing how dark corruption (Darksphere) can compromise trusted authority figures, forcing the hero into a position of fighting former allies. Faction identity relies less on color-coding and more on narrative context, unit type design, and positional relationships on the battlefield.
